stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML domains like transformers and text-generation.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, secure model artifact usage.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate its relevance within the Hugging Face ecosystem (transformers, safetensors).

stories.title: Apple takes Epic fight over app store fees to the Supreme Court
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Apple is escalating its legal battle with Epic Games to the Supreme Court.
The core issue before the Supreme Court is whether a prior contempt finding against Apple in the Epic case was legally erroneous.
This case represents a major legal challenge to Apple's control over its App Store fee structure and ecosystem.

stories.title: FARS: A Fully Automated Research System Deployed at Scale
stories.author: Yunfan Shao
bullet_points: Introduces FARS, a novel Fully Automated Research System designed for AI-for-AI research.
FARS aims to overcome limitations of current automated systems by operating across diverse research topics at scale.
The system autonomously manages the entire research lifecycle, from hypothesis generation and planning to experimentation and manuscript writing.

stories.title: A Lifecycle and Application-Stack Survey of Large Language Model Vulnerabilities: Attacks, Risks, Defenses, and Open Problems
stories.author: Bo Tang
bullet_points: LLMs are moving beyond simple text generation and are being integrated into complex, high-stakes systems (e.g., robotics, autonomous agents, enterprise assistants).
The security risk has shifted from just the model weights to the entire 'lifecycle and application stack' through which the model operates.
The expanded capabilities of LLMs—such as reading private data, calling tools, and executing code—introduce new, critical security vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Moral Safety in LLMs: Exposing Performative Compliance with Puzzled Cues
stories.author: Yulia Tsvetkov
bullet_points: Examines the superficial nature of ethical behavior in Large Language Models (LLMs) when deployed in sensitive fields like healthcare and hiring.
Introduces the concept of 'performative compliance,' where models exhibit fairness only when demographic identity is explicitly labeled.
Demonstrates that current fairness evaluations overestimate a model's true moral safety when identity must be inferred by the model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, suggesting a focus on accessible LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Faster Gemma 4 on MLX with multi-token prediction
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Gemma 4 inference speed is significantly improved within Ollama 0.31 on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is achieved through the implementation of Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) powered by MLX.
Coding agent tasks show up to 90% faster performance according to the Aider polyglot benchmark.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model responses.
The updated engine also delivers faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new model.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing local AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating active development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring data processing occurs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing powerful, private AI assistance without reliance on cloud services.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, via local or cloud deployment.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A new attack vector demonstrates the vulnerability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to prompt injection.
The attack exploits the LLM's susceptibility to seemingly innocuous, misleading inputs (e.g., '2 + 2 = 5').
The finding serves as a cautionary tale against the deployment of AI-integrated browsers due to inherent security risks.

stories.title: Surrogate Fidelity: When Can Open LLMs Explain Closed Ones?
stories.author: Nehal Bandi
bullet_points: The paper addresses the 'surrogate problem' in mechanistic interpretability (MI) when analyzing closed, proprietary LLMs.
It investigates the limits of using open LLMs as proxies to understand the internal workings of closed models, given API constraints.
The research evaluates 'surrogate fidelity' across prediction, attribution, and representation levels using methods like log-odds.

stories.title: CLExEval: A Human-in-the-Loop Framework for Qualitative Evaluation of LLM Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Zhuohan Xie
bullet_points: Introduces CLExEval, a new human-in-the-loop framework specifically designed to evaluate LLM clinical reasoning.
Addresses the 'evaluation illusion' risk where fluent LLM explanations can mask incorrect clinical diagnoses.
Utilizes a methodology involving progressive information masking and leverages 5,600 expert-physician annotations.

stories.title: AutoTrainess: Teaching Language Models to Improve Language Models Autonomously
stories.author: Xiao-Ping Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces AutoTrainess, a system designed to enable language models to autonomously improve other language models.
Addresses the human-intensive nature of LM training by automating complex post-training tasks.
The system manages the entire iterative training loop, including data construction, job execution, and checkpoint evaluation.

stories.title: RaBitQCache: Rotated Binary Quantization for KVCache in Long Context LLM Inference
stories.author: Xiaoyong Du
bullet_points: Addresses the KV cache bottleneck in long-context LLM inference, a major industry challenge.
Introduces RaBitQCache, a novel sparse attention framework using randomized rotated binary quantization.
Leverages high-throughput binary-INT4 arithmetic to efficiently estimate attention weights, improving inference speed.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these AI agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for more complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing the model's access to real-time information.

stories.title: Meta is adding ridiculous ‘rate limits’ and a soft paywall to its smart glasses
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Meta is implementing rate limits on the Conversation Focus feature of its smart glasses.
Access to the feature will be limited to three hours per month by default.
A paid subscription, Meta One Premium ($19.99/month), is required to remove these usage restrictions.

stories.title: Anthropic’s long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Anthropic is resuming access to its Claude Fable 5 model.
The resumption follows the lifting of export controls by the Department of Commerce.
The decision was reportedly reached after negotiations involving the Trump administration.

stories.title: Dish files for bankruptcy, but not shutting down
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Dish has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection.
The filing allows the company to continue operating rather than shutting down.
The bankruptcy is linked to delays in selling $23 billion of 5G spectrum to AT&T.

stories.title: Amazon fined $2.25 million for failing to help identity theft victims
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon was fined $2.25 million by the Federal Trade Commission (FTC).
The fine relates to the company's failure to assist customers who were victims of identity theft.
The FTC alleged Amazon refused to provide customers with details regarding purchases made using fraudulent accounts.

stories.title: Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Google's NotebookLM is introducing a new feature to generate short, TikTok-style AI videos from user-uploaded research notes.
This capability is currently rolling out to subscribers of Google AI Ultra and Pro.
The feature aims to make synthesizing and catching up on complex research material more digestible and engaging.

stories.title: Netflix is using an AI-generated Gene Wilder voice in its Willy Wonka reality show
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: Netflix is utilizing AI-generated voice technology for its upcoming Willy Wonka reality show, 'Wonka's The Golden Ticket'.
The show follows a trend of fictional competition formats, similar to 'Squid Game'.
While the physical sets are real, the use of AI voiceover highlights the integration of generative AI in entertainment production.

stories.title: Reddit will require you to log in to use old.reddit.com
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Reddit is implementing a login requirement for access to the legacy old.reddit.com interface.
The primary motivation for this change is to combat abusive scraping activities.
This represents a shift in how Reddit manages access to its older web properties.

stories.title: Amazon blames piracy apps with malware for killing new Fire Stick sideloading
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Amazon is implementing changes to the Fire Stick OS to restrict third-party applications.
The changes aim to block features like third-party homepage launchers and ad blockers.
Amazon attributes the previous issues with sideloading to malware distributed via piracy apps.

stories.title: NASA may send a backup, nuclear-powered Mars rover to the Moon
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA is considering sending a backup, nuclear-powered Mars rover to the Moon.
The concept involves repurposing advanced Mars technology for lunar missions.
The potential capability is described as 'awesome' by observers.

stories.title: Google kills Tenor GIF API, forcing changes at X, Discord, and more
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google has discontinued its Tenor GIF API, impacting third-party integrations.
Platforms like X and Discord are now required to find alternative sources for GIF functionality.
Tenor's connection to Google applications remains functional, but external API access is restricted.

stories.title: ECHO: Prune to act, trace to learn with selective turn memory in agentic RL
stories.author: Zeyu Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon language agents needing to manage vast interaction histories.
Proposes 'ECHO,' a method using selective turn memory to balance action execution and learning.
Overcomes limitations of current context management techniques like truncation and summarization.

stories.title: Think in English, Answer in Korean: Efficient Adaptation of Multilingual Tool-Using Agents
stories.author: Susie Park
bullet_points: Introduction of LuckyStar 111B, a 111B-parameter hybrid reasoning model designed for enterprise agents.
The model focuses on efficient multilingual operation, specifically handling English input and Korean output.
It utilizes transfer learning from Cohere's Command A model and employs preamble conditioning for switching between reasoning and non-reasoning tasks.

stories.title: Intrinsic decomposition and editing of 3D Gaussian splats
stories.author: George Drettakis
bullet_points: The research extends the concept of intrinsic decomposition (separating color into albedo and shading) to 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS).
Intrinsic decomposition allows for editing object colors and textures independently of scene lighting, a key feature in image editing.
The paper proposes solutions to model this intrinsic decomposition specifically within the framework of radiance fields represented by Gaussian splats.

stories.title: A Tutorial on Autonomous Fault-Tolerant Control Using Knowledge-Grounded LLM Agents
stories.author: Mehmet Mercangöz
bullet_points: Proposes using Knowledge-Grounded LLM Agents to assist in autonomous fault-tolerant control for process plants.
Addresses the current reliance on human operators to interpret complex plant data (alarms, P&IDs, trends) during faults.
Frames the LLM as a constrained supervisory planner to guide recovery decisions outside of standard logic.

stories.title: Automating Cause-Effect Specification with Knowledge Graphs and Large Language Models
stories.author: Mehmet Mercangöz
bullet_points: The research addresses the manual and error-prone nature of creating critical process control specifications (like C&E matrices).
It proposes a novel semantic-AI framework that automates C&E logic generation by integrating a Knowledge Graph (KG) with a constrained Large Language Model (LLM).
The framework leverages an established modular alignment ontology to structure the knowledge base for automated reasoning.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates whether training Language Models (LMs) to generate explanations leads to genuine 'faithful introspection' or just superficial imitation.
The study uses a novel supervision method based on the model's counterfactual behavior when inputs are slightly modified.
Key findings suggest that LMs can be trained effectively using fixed counterfactual explanations derived from past checkpoints or similar models.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
Proposes QVal, a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals for intermediate actions.
Focuses on improving guidance for LLMs by moving beyond relying solely on final outcome rewards.

stories.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
stories.author: Chelsea Finn
bullet_points: Addresses the critical bottleneck of reward design in autonomous robot policy improvement.
Introduces Freeform Preference Learning (FPL) to learn robot policies from human preferences.
FPL allows annotators to provide nuanced feedback beyond simple binary comparisons of trajectories.

stories.title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces AdaJEPA, an adaptive latent world model designed to improve planning capabilities.
Addresses the critical issue of model failure during test-time distribution shift in latent world models.
Integrates test-time adaptation directly within the closed-loop framework of Model Predictive Control (MPC).

stories.title: Automated Background Swapping for Robustness against Spurious Backgrounds
stories.author: Kajetan Schweighofer
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of spurious correlations in Deep Neural Networks, where models rely on non-causal features.
Focuses specifically on the vision domain, where backgrounds often introduce these misleading correlations.
Proposes an automated method for background swapping to enhance model robustness and generalization.

stories.title: TRIAGE: Role-Typed Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) where credit assignment is based only on the final outcome.
It introduces TRIAGE, a novel role-typed credit assignment framework specifically for Agentic Reinforcement Learning.
TRIAGE aims to provide a more nuanced reward signal, preventing the penalization of useful exploration and reinforcing redundant actions.

stories.title: FedLAB: Traceable Semantic Codebooks for Federated Multimodal Graph Foundation Learning
stories.author: Guoren Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of training multimodal graph foundation models when data is decentralized due to privacy concerns.
Proposes 'FedLAB,' a method for federated multimodal graph foundation learning.
Focuses on using 'Traceable Semantic Codebooks' to enable knowledge sharing across distributed clients.

stories.title: Tone-Conditioned Curriculum Learning for Low-Resource Bantu Speech Recognition
stories.author: Thapelo Sindane
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of high Word Error Rates (WER > 100%) in zero-shot ASR for low-resource Southern Bantu languages.
Introduces a novel 'tone-conditioned curriculum framework' combining hybrid difficulty scoring and gated adapters driven by tonal statistics.
Demonstrates practical application by training on a community corpus and testing robustness via transfer learning to NCHLT.

stories.title: Robust Text Watermarking for Large Language Models via Dual Semantic Embeddings
stories.author: Gerhard Wunder
bullet_points: Introduces Dual-Embedding Watermarking (DEW), a new semantic watermarking scheme for LLMs.
DEW enhances watermark robustness against paraphrasing and translation by using both contextual and token-level embeddings.
The method employs signal-processing and algebraic vector-space operations to create a watermark signal that degrades gracefully under semantic shifts.

stories.title: Modality-Driven Search with Holistic Trace Judging for ARC-AGI-2
stories.author: Johan Land
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of LLMs being confidently wrong in abstract reasoning, shifting focus from generation to candidate selection.
A new solver for the ARC-AGI-2 visual reasoning benchmark is proposed, leveraging multimodal reasoning.
The approach treats reasoning modalities (text, image, code) as independent search operators to generate diverse candidates.

stories.title: FinPersona-Bench: A Benchmark for Longitudinal Psychometric Stability of Autonomous Financial Agents
stories.author: Zhuohan Xie
bullet_points: Introduces FinPersona-Bench, a new benchmark designed to test the long-term behavioral consistency of autonomous financial agents powered by LLMs.
Identifies and formalizes 'Mandate Salience Decay' (MSD), the problem where initial behavioral rules (e.g., 'preserve capital') degrade over long-term market interactions.
Addresses a critical reliability and governance challenge in deploying LLMs for high-stakes financial decision-making.

stories.title: Building an ASR Solution for Training and Assessing Children's Reading
stories.author: Michael Leventhal
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) for children's reading in under-resourced African languages, specifically Bambara.
Presents an end-to-end, open-source system integrating field data collection, model adaptation, and classroom validation.
The research utilized a mobile application to collect 55 hours of relevant speech data for the assessment system.

stories.title: Claude Code is steganographically marking requests
stories.author: kirushik
bullet_points: Claude Code, an AI model, is reportedly using steganography to mark its requests.
This suggests a hidden or covert communication mechanism within the model's operations.
The finding raises questions about data privacy, model transparency, and potential backdoors.

stories.title: Claude Code Is Steganographically Marking Requests
stories.author: thereallo.dev via alexjs
bullet_points: The news concerns a technical observation regarding Claude AI's code.
The core finding is that Claude is allegedly using steganography to mark requests.
This suggests potential hidden metadata or signaling within the AI's interactions.
